title: Protecting Your Income: Insurance Basics
concept: Health, term, accident & critical illness cover
summary: Insurance protects your savings from large, rare shocks. For a young earner, health insurance and an emergency fund usually matter first; term insurance matters most once people depend on your income.

sections.heading: Why protection before growth
sections.body: One hospital stay or accident can wipe out years of saving. Insurance transfers that rare-but-huge risk to an insurer for a small, predictable premium.

sections.heading: The four common covers
sections.body: Health insurance pays hospital bills. Personal accident cover pays if an accident affects your ability to earn. Critical illness cover pays a lump sum on serious diagnoses. Term insurance pays your dependents if you die.

sections.heading: What a fresher usually needs first
sections.body: Without dependents, term insurance is often not urgent. Your own health cover (don't rely only on employer cover) and an emergency fund usually come first.

options: Protect your savings from rare, large shocks
You are young with no dependents
People financially depend on your income

quiz.prompt: Which cover pays your dependents if you die?
For a fresher with no dependents, what usually comes first?
options: Their own health cover and an emergency fund
A critical illness lump-sum plan only

quiz.prompt: Why is it unwise to rely only on employer-provided health cover?
options: Employer cover always pays more than you need
It can end or fall short when you change or lose the job
It is illegal to use employer cover

disclaimer: Educational content only — not advice to buy any specific policy. Consult a licensed professional for real decisions.
